title: Hours of work, breaks and rosters
content:
Hours of work, breaks and rosters

Awards, enterprise agreements and other registered agreements have rules about an employee's hours of work, breaks, rosters and rostered days off.

Ordinary hours: Ordinary hours are the hours worked by an employee that don't include overtime. The time of the day ordinary hours are worked is called the spread of hours (for example, between 7am and 7pm). Time worked outside the spread of ordinary hours can attract overtime rates.

When overtime applies: Overtime is when an employee works extra time. It can include time worked:
- on top of the ordinary hours of work
- outside the agreed number of hours
- outside the spread of ordinary hours

Right to disconnect: Employees have the right to refuse contact outside their working hours, unless doing so is unreasonable. This means an employee can refuse to monitor, read or respond to contact (or attempted contact) from an employer or a third party. Awards, enterprise agreements and other registered agreements can also include additional right to disconnect provisions.

Breaks: Awards, enterprise agreements and other registered agreements provide for paid and unpaid rest breaks and meal breaks. Awards and registered agreements can also provide for a minimum amount of time off between the end of one shift and the start of another.

Rosters: A roster is a timetable that shows the days and times employees are required to work. Awards, enterprise agreements and other registered agreements can set rules about changing rosters and how and when employees are given rosters.

Rostered days off (RDO): A rostered day off is a day in a roster period that an employee doesn't have to work. An employee's day off can be paid or unpaid, depending on how RDOs are set out in an award or registered agreement.

key_facts.ordinary_hours_set_by: Award, enterprise agreement, or employment contract
key_facts.spread_of_hours_definition: Times of day during which ordinary hours can be worked
key_facts.rostered_days_off_rdo_applies: Where award or agreement provides for them
key_facts.overtime_trigger: Work outside ordinary hours as defined in award/agreement
